title: Component graph webview is 'the brain': always-dark canvas, memory-density heat (not fragility)
context: The VS Code extension's Component Graph is Hunch's adoption/demo hero (per dec_8dc263b43e 'hero graph alone suffices'). The prior SVG version rendered flat theme-colored circles with node color = fragility. But in real graphs the component fragility field is near-always 0.0, so a fragility color ramp renders every node identical — no visual signal, no 'wow'.
decision: Rewrote graph.ts from theme-driven SVG to a self-contained <canvas> 'brain' matching the site hero's constellation aesthetic (moss/ember palette, IBM Plex Mono, glow, breathing embers, curved dendritic synapses, hover-neighborhood illumination, traveling glints). Two deliberate encoding choices: (1) node HEAT = memory density = decisions + constraints*2 + bugs*3 (how much Hunch KNOWS about a component — the product story, and it varies 2..35 unlike fragility); node SIZE = owned symbols; guarded (constraint-in-scope) nodes breathe as moss embers; link glow/width = cross-component call weight. (2) The canvas is ALWAYS dark ('void' #0e1512), deliberately ignoring VS Code's light/dark theme — glow is invisible on a light background, and the dark void is the intended brain aesthetic + gives consistent marketing screenshots. acquireVsCodeApi() is typeof-guarded so the HTML also renders in a plain browser (used for offline screenshot verification). Extension stays a pure JSON reader — no writes, no data-layer change; showGraph/refreshGraph signatures unchanged.
consequences: The graph panel is dark even in a light VS Code theme (intentional).
Visual encoding now reflects Hunch's memory density, so a well-documented component literally glows brighter — reinforces the product story.
alternatives_rejected: Keep node color = fragility (renders all-identical because fragility is ~0 everywhere)
Adapt canvas to VS Code theme (a light-theme variant kills the glow that makes it read as a brain)
Add glow via SVG feGaussianBlur instead of canvas (janky and slow for breathing/trails/hover)
